file_summary: This JSON file stores requirement tracking data for a software project. It contains a hierarchical structure with a 'workspaces' key that maps to a specific directory path (C:\Users\14636\Desktop\DevBrain). Under that path is an array of requirement objects, each representing a tracked feature or task. Each requirement has fields for ID, description text, completion status, notes, timestamps, and a source thread identifier. The data shows 17 requirements, with the first 14 marked as completed and the last 3 as pending.

line_windows.summary: Lines 1-80 contain the opening of the JSON structure, the 'workspaces' object, and the beginning of the requirement array for the DevBrain directory. This window includes the first 8 requirement objects (req-0001 through req-0008). Each requirement has a descriptive text in Chinese, a 'completed' status, a note indicating implementation, and timestamps. The lines show the consistent structure of each requirement object within the array.

Lines 61-140 contain a middle segment of the requirement array, overlapping with the end of window 0. This window includes requirement objects req-0007 through req-0014. It shows the continuation of completed requirements with similar structure, ending with req-0014 which describes the creation of a 'requirement-tracker skill' with features like completion summaries and session display. The lines maintain the same JSON object format for each requirement entry.

Lines 121-159 contain the final portion of the requirement array and the closing of the JSON structure. This window includes the last 3 requirement objects (req-0015 through req-0017), all marked with 'pending' status and notes indicating they are historical backlog items awaiting implementation. Unlike previous requirements, these have different updated_at timestamps and req-0017 has identical created_at and updated_at values. The window ends with the closing brackets for the requirement array, the workspaces object, and the entire JSON document.
